passage: An academic support team proposes expanding tutoring and structured note-review sessions. Team members claim that students using these supports score higher, on average, than students who do not use them.
question: Which choice is best supported by the chart?

choices.A: Students who attended tutoring scored higher than students with no tutoring, and students who reviewed notes scored higher than those who did not.
choices.B: Only tutoring appears related to higher scores; note review appears unrelated.
choices.C: Students who did not review notes scored higher than those who did, but tutoring still helped.
choices.D: The highest-scoring group was students with no tutoring, indicating tutoring was ineffective.

explanation: The chart shows 79 vs 71 for tutoring/no tutoring and 74 vs 68 for review/no review. In both comparisons, the support condition is associated with a higher average score, supporting choice A.
